description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a mix of dark gray, greenish, and brown hues. Prominent white and tan mineral veins run diagonally across its surface, giving it a striking, layered appearance. The rock's texture appears coarse and uneven, with jagged edges and rough surfaces that suggest it was naturally fractured. Its coloration and mineral banding indicate it could be a metamorphic rock, such as gneiss or schist, commonly found in landscaped urban environments. The rock is solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings. Its size and shape make it suitable as a natural barrier or decorative element in parks, gardens, or along sidewalks.
description: A rugged, angular rock with dark gray, greenish, and brown tones, featuring prominent white and tan mineral veins and a coarse, jagged texture.
description_view_0: The rock appears triangular with a dark, shadowed face and visible white mineral streaks along one edge.
description_view_1: This side highlights a broad, slanted surface with pronounced brown and white banding running vertically.
description_view_2: The rock looks taller and narrower, with a mostly gray-green surface and subtle white veining.
description_view_3: A wide, sloped face is visible, dominated by a thick white mineral vein and a mix of gray and green coloration.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
